message:
ci: Add GitHub Actions CI/CD workflows

Add comprehensive CI/CD pipeline for rich_rust library:

- ci.yml: Build, test, clippy lint, and rustfmt check on push/PR
  - Feature matrix testing (default, syntax, markdown, json, full)
  - Multi-OS testing (ubuntu, macos, windows)
  - Uses nightly toolchain for Rust 2024 edition

- release.yml: Cross-platform release builds triggered on version tags
  - Builds library artifacts for linux-x64, linux-arm64, macos-x64, macos-arm64, windows-x64
  - Generates SHA256 checksums for all artifacts
  - Creates GitHub release with archives

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"rich_rust-8ut","title":"Implement cell_len Caching for Performance","description":"## Objective
Add caching to cell_len() calculations for improved performance.

## Background
cell_len() calculates the display width of strings in terminal cells. It's called extensively during text wrapping, table layout, and alignment. Caching can significantly improve performance.

## Technical Details

### Current State
- cell_len() iterates over every character, calling get_character_cell_size()
- No caching of computed widths
- Repeated calls for same strings recompute from scratch

### Proposed Implementation
Two-level caching:
1. Character-level: cache width of individual chars (probably already via unicode-width)
2. String-level: cache total width of frequently-used strings

```rust
use std::sync::Mutex;
use lru::LruCache;

lazy_static! {
    static ref WIDTH_CACHE: Mutex<LruCache<String, usize>> = 
        Mutex::new(LruCache::new(1024));
}

pub fn cell_len_cached(s: &str) -> usize {
    // Short strings: compute directly (cache overhead not worth it)
    if s.len() < 8 {
        return cell_len(s);
    }
    
    let mut cache = WIDTH_CACHE.lock().unwrap();
    if let Some(&width) = cache.get(s) {
        return width;
    }
    let width = cell_len(s);
    cache.put(s.to_string(), width);
    width
}
```

### Considerations
- Cache invalidation: not needed (width is deterministic)
- Memory: bound cache size to prevent growth
- Short string threshold: benchmark to find optimal cutoff
- Thread safety: use appropriate synchronization

## Files to Modify
- src/cells.rs (add caching)
- Cargo.toml (lru dependency if not already added)

## Testing
- Benchmark table rendering with/without cache
- Test with various text sizes
- Verify thread safety","acceptance_criteria":"cell_len caching implemented. Benchmarks show measurable improvement.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:21:13.116410969-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:21:13.116410969-05:00","labels":["implementation","performance"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-8ut","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-a96","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:22:55.897283402-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"rich_rust-8ut","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-yz6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:25:08.598353178-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"rich_rust-8ut","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-cwe","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:43:23.076534202-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-a62","title":"Verify Style Parsing Edge Cases","description":"## Objective
Ensure Style::parse() handles all edge cases from RICH_SPEC.md Section 2.

## Background
Style parsing converts strings like 'bold red on blue' into Style structs. Edge cases include empty strings, invalid keywords, and complex combinations.

## Technical Details

### Test Cases from Spec

#### Valid Inputs
- '' (empty) → null style
- 'bold' → bold attribute only
- 'red' → red foreground only
- 'on blue' → blue background only
- 'bold italic red on blue' → all combined
- 'not bold' → unset bold
- '#ff0000' → hex color foreground
- 'rgb(255,0,0)' → RGB color foreground
- 'color(128)' → 256-color foreground
- 'link=https://example.com' → hyperlink

#### Invalid Inputs (should error or ignore)
- 'invalid' → unknown keyword
- 'bold bold' → duplicate attribute
- 'on' → incomplete background
- 'red blue' → ambiguous (two foregrounds?)

### Verification Steps
1. Write test for each valid input
2. Write test for each invalid input
3. Compare error handling to Python Rich
4. Verify error messages are helpful

## Files to Examine
- src/style.rs (parse method)
- Python Rich style.py (reference)
- RICH_SPEC.md Section 2.4","acceptance_criteria":"All edge cases handled correctly. Error messages are helpful.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:22:21.885748221-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:22:21.885748221-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-a62","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-a96","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:23:03.886973112-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"rich_rust-a62","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-kko","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:43:22.941197777-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-a96","title":"EPIC: Implementation Refinement & Gap Closure","description":"## Objective
Address identified gaps between current implementation and RICH_SPEC requirements, plus implement missing features discovered during validation.

## Background
During spec validation, several gaps and missing features were identified. This epic tracks all implementation work needed to achieve full spec compliance.

## Scope

### Critical Gaps (Must Fix)
1. LRU caching for color parsing - spec requires caching for performance
2. Text.divide() algorithm - must match Python Rich exactly
3. Ratio distribution algorithm - verify integer rounding behavior
4. Hyperlink support (OSC 8) - may be incomplete

### Potential Gaps (Verify First)
1. Style parsing edge cases
2. Markup parser error recovery
3. Table width collapse algorithm
4. Wide character handling in cell calculations

### Implementation Improvements
1. API ergonomics (builder patterns, From impls)
2. Error messages and handling
3. Thread safety considerations

## Success Criteria
- All RICH_SPEC requirements implemented
- No behavioral differences from Python Rich (for in-scope features)
- Clean, idiomatic Rust code

## Dependencies
- Depends on Spec Validation epic completing to identify all gaps","acceptance_criteria":"All identified gaps closed. Spec compliance verified.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:19:54.039911917-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:19:54.039911917-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-a96","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-q1k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:23:04.859808161-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
-{"id":"rich_rust-ag4","title":"Create E2E Test: Text Wrapping and Justification","description":"## Objective
E2E tests for text wrapping, justification, and the Text.divide() algorithm.

## Test Scenarios

### Scenario 1: Basic Word Wrap
```rust
#[test]
fn test_e2e_word_wrap() {
    let _guard = init_test_logging();
    
    let text = Text::plain("The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog")
        .wrap(20);
    
    let lines: Vec<_> = text.iter().collect();
    debug!(line_count = lines.len(), "Wrapped text");
    
    for (i, line) in lines.iter().enumerate() {
        let width = line.cell_length();
        debug!(line = i, width = width, content = %line, "Line detail");
        assert!(width <= 20, "Line {} exceeds width: {}", i, width);
    }
    
    info!("Word wrap test PASSED");
}
```

### Scenario 2: Long Word Breaking
- Word longer than width
- Verify: Word is broken, not truncated

### Scenario 3: Styled Text Wrapping
- Text with style spans
- Verify: Styles preserved across line breaks

### Scenario 4: Text.divide() Algorithm (CRITICAL)
```rust
#[test]
fn test_e2e_text_divide() {
    let _guard = init_test_logging();
    
    let mut text = Text::plain("hello world!");
    text.stylize(6, 11, Style::new().bold());  // 'world' is bold
    
    debug!(original = %text.plain, spans = ?text.spans, "Original text");
    
    let divided = text.divide(&[5, 10]);
    
    for (i, part) in divided.iter().enumerate() {
        debug!(
            part = i,
            content = %part.plain,
            spans = ?part.spans,
            "Divided part"
        );
    }
    
    // Verify spans are correctly split
    assert_eq!(divided[0].plain, "hello");
    assert!(divided[0].spans.is_empty(), "First part should have no spans");
    
    assert_eq!(divided[1].plain, " worl");
    assert!(!divided[1].spans.is_empty(), "Second part should have bold span");
    
    assert_eq!(divided[2].plain, "d!");
    assert!(!divided[2].spans.is_empty(), "Third part should have bold span");
    
    info!("Text.divide test PASSED");
}
```

### Scenario 5: Justification
- Left, Right, Center, Full justify
- Verify: Correct padding/spacing

### Scenario 6: Wide Characters
- CJK text wrapping
- Emoji handling
- Verify: Cell width respected

### Scenario 7: Overflow Methods
- Fold, Crop, Ellipsis
- Verify: Each method works correctly

## Logging Requirements
- Original text and spans
- Wrap width and resulting lines
- Divide offsets and resulting parts
- Span transformations during divide
- Cell width calculations

## Files to Create
- tests/e2e_text.rs
- tests/golden/text_*.txt","acceptance_criteria":"All text scenarios pass. divide() algorithm verified with span preservation.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:45:03.615570733-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:45:03.615570733-05:00","labels":["e2e","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-ag4","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-6d6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:45:12.972287839-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"rich_rust-ag4","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-4ot","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T11:11:54.099529002-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

 {"id":"rich_rust-agw","title":"Add Comprehensive Rustdoc Comments","description":"## Objective
Add complete rustdoc documentation to all public APIs.

## Documentation Standards

### Module Documentation
Each module (color, style, text, etc.) should have:
- Overview of module purpose
- Key types/functions summary
- Usage example
- Cross-references to related modules

### Struct Documentation
Each public struct should have:
- Purpose description
- Fields documentation
- Example construction
- Common use cases

### Function Documentation
Each public function should have:
- Brief description
- Parameters explanation
- Return value
- Panics section (if applicable)
- Example usage
- Errors section (for Result returns)

### Code Examples
- All examples must compile (use ```rust or ```rust,ignore)
- Show common use cases
- Demonstrate error handling where relevant

## Files to Document
- [ ] src/lib.rs (crate overview)
- [ ] src/color.rs
- [ ] src/style.rs
- [ ] src/segment.rs
- [ ] src/text.rs
- [ ] src/markup/mod.rs
- [ ] src/measure.rs
- [ ] src/console.rs
- [ ] src/box.rs
- [ ] src/cells.rs
- [ ] src/renderables/*.rs

## Verification
- Run `cargo doc --no-deps` successfully
- No missing docs warnings with #![warn(missing_docs)]
- Examples in docs compile","acceptance_criteria":"All public APIs documented. cargo doc builds cleanly.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:25:35.808638033-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:25:35.808638033-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-agw","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-2oy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:25:47.917432688-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-b7s","title":"EPIC: End-to-End Testing & Validation Infrastructure","description":"## Objective
Create comprehensive end-to-end test infrastructure with detailed logging to ensure rich_rust works correctly as a complete system.

## Background
Unit tests verify individual components, but E2E tests verify the entire rendering pipeline works correctly from user input to terminal output. This is critical for a terminal rendering library where visual correctness matters.

## Why E2E Tests Are Critical
1. **Integration verification**: Components that pass unit tests may fail when combined
2. **Visual regression detection**: Catch rendering changes that affect appearance
3. **Performance regression detection**: Catch slowdowns in real-world scenarios
4. **Cross-platform validation**: Ensure consistent behavior across environments

## Scope

### Test Infrastructure
- Test harness with detailed logging (using tracing crate)
- Golden file comparison for visual output
- Performance regression detection
- CI integration with artifact preservation

### Test Scenarios
- Complete rendering pipeline tests
- Complex document rendering
- Edge case combinations
- Performance benchmarks as tests

### Logging Requirements
- Structured logging with tracing
- Test-specific log levels
- Timing information for each phase
- Memory usage tracking
- Diff output for failures

## Success Criteria
- All major user scenarios covered by E2E tests
- Detailed logs available for debugging failures
- CI runs tests and preserves artifacts
- Performance baselines established and monitored","acceptance_criteria":"E2E test suite operational. All scenarios pass. Logs are detailed and useful.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:43:43.32014929-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:43:43.32014929-05:00"}

@@ -51,7 +51,7 @@
 {"id":"rich_rust-nqh","title":"Create Migration Guide from Python Rich","description":"## Objective
Create a migration guide helping Python Rich users adopt rich_rust.

## Guide Contents

### Feature Mapping Table
| Python Rich | rich_rust | Notes |
|-------------|-----------|-------|
| console.print() | Console::print() | Same semantics |
| Text() | Text::new() | Similar API |
| Style() | Style::new() | Builder pattern |
| Table() | Table::new() | Similar API |
| Panel() | Panel::new() | Similar API |
| Markdown() | Markdown::parse() | Feature flag |
| Syntax() | Syntax::new() | Feature flag |
| Live() | Not implemented | Excluded |
| Status() | Not implemented | Excluded |

### API Differences
- Python: console.print(text, style='bold')
- Rust: console.print('[bold]text[/]');

### Markup Syntax
- Same: [bold], [red], [on blue], [/]
- Same: Escaping with backslash

### Console Options
- force_terminal -> force_terminal
- record -> record
- width -> width()
- color_system -> color_system()

### Common Patterns

Python:
```python
console = Console()
table = Table(title='My Table')
table.add_column('Name')
table.add_row('Alice')
console.print(table)
```

Rust:
```rust
let console = Console::new();
let table = Table::new()
    .title('My Table')
    .add_column(Column::new('Name'))
    .add_row(Row::new(vec\![Cell::new('Alice')]));
console.print(table);
```

### Feature Flags
- syntax: Syntax highlighting (requires syntect)
- markdown: Markdown rendering (requires pulldown-cmark)
- json: JSON rendering (requires serde_json)

## Files to Create
- docs/MIGRATION.md","acceptance_criteria":"Migration guide covers all shared features. Code examples work.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:25:37.84434352-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:25:37.84434352-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-nqh","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-2oy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:25:48.024395587-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-p8z","title":"Validate Segment Operations against RICH_SPEC Section 3","description":"## Objective
Verify the Segment implementation in src/segment.rs matches RICH_SPEC.md Section 3 exactly.

## Background
Segment is the atomic rendering unit - a piece of text with a single style. The rendering pipeline produces streams of segments. Control segments handle terminal manipulation (cursor, clear, etc.).

## Validation Checklist

### 3.1 Segment Struct
- [ ] Verify fields: text (String), style (Option<Style>), control (Option<Vec<ControlCode>>)
- [ ] Verify Segment::new(text, style) constructor
- [ ] Verify Segment::styled(text, style) convenience constructor
- [ ] Verify Segment::plain(text) for unstyled text
- [ ] Verify Segment::line() creates newline segment
- [ ] Verify Segment::control(codes) creates control segment

### 3.2 ControlType Enum
- [ ] Verify all control types: Bell, CarriageReturn, Home, Clear, ShowCursor, HideCursor, EnableAltScreen, DisableAltScreen, CursorUp, CursorDown, CursorForward, CursorBackward, CursorMoveToColumn, CursorMoveTo, EraseInLine, SetWindowTitle
- [ ] Verify ControlCode struct with control_type and params fields

### 3.3 Segment Methods
- [ ] Verify is_control() returns true iff control is Some
- [ ] Verify cell_length() returns 0 for control segments
- [ ] Verify cell_length() uses Unicode cell width for text
- [ ] Verify is_empty() checks both text and control
- [ ] Verify split_at_cell(pos) splits at cell position (not byte)

### 3.4 Segment Operations (Functions)
- [ ] Verify apply_style(segments, style, post_style)
- [ ] Verify split_lines(segments) splits at newlines
- [ ] Verify adjust_line_length(line, length, style, pad)
- [ ] Verify simplify(segments) merges adjacent same-style
- [ ] Verify divide(segments, cuts) divides at cell positions
- [ ] Verify align_top/bottom/middle vertical alignment

### 3.5 Edge Cases
- [ ] Test split_at_cell with wide characters (CJK, emoji)
- [ ] Test divide with cuts at wide character boundaries
- [ ] Test simplify preserves control segments

## Files to Examine
- src/segment.rs (primary)
- src/cells.rs (cell width calculation)
- RICH_SPEC.md Section 3","acceptance_criteria":"All checklist items verified. Edge case handling verified.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:16:52.788032827-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:16:52.788032827-05:00","labels":["phase-1","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-p8z","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-q1k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:19:35.749658257-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-q1k","title":"EPIC: Spec Validation & Implementation Alignment","description":"# EPIC: Spec Validation & Implementation Alignment

## Overview
Comprehensive validation of the Rust implementation against RICH_SPEC.md to ensure behavioral parity with Python Rich library.

## Background & Reasoning
The rich_rust project is a port of Python's Rich library to Rust. The RICH_SPEC.md document contains the complete behavioral specification extracted from Python Rich source (v13.x). This epic ensures:

1. **Behavioral Correctness**: Every feature works exactly as specified
2. **Edge Case Coverage**: Boundary conditions handled correctly  
3. **Algorithm Fidelity**: Complex algorithms (color conversion, ratio distribution, text division) match spec
4. **API Completeness**: All documented methods and properties exist

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All spec sections validated against implementation
- [ ] Discrepancies documented and resolved
- [ ] Test coverage for each validated behavior
- [ ] No regressions in existing functionality

## Child Tasks (by module)
1. Color System validation
2. Style System validation
3. Segment operations validation
4. Markup parser validation
5. Measurement protocol validation
6. Text object validation (CRITICAL: division algorithm)
7. Console validation
8. Box characters validation
9. Table layout validation (CRITICAL: width calculation)
10. Panel & Padding validation
11. Alignment system validation
12. Unicode cell width validation
13. Text wrapping validation
14. Ratio distribution validation

## Dependencies
- Blocks: All implementation refinement tasks
- Blocks: All performance optimization tasks

## Technical Notes
- Use the RICH_SPEC.md as the source of truth
- Create property-based tests where applicable
- Document any intentional deviations from spec","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:13:29.484048364-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T11:25:35.812552966-05:00"}
-{"id":"rich_rust-q5f","title":"Validate Table Width Algorithms against RICH_SPEC Section 9.4","description":"## Objective
Verify Table width calculation algorithms match RICH_SPEC.md Section 9.4 EXACTLY.

## Background
This is the CRITICAL algorithm validation for Table. Width calculation is complex and must match spec precisely to avoid layout bugs.

## Validation Checklist

### get_row_widths()
- [ ] Measures each cell in row
- [ ] Returns Vec<Measurement> with min/max per column
- [ ] Accounts for padding
- [ ] Handles multi-line cells correctly

### calculate_widths() Main Algorithm
- [ ] Step 1: Get minimum and maximum widths per column
- [ ] Step 2: Calculate total minimum width
- [ ] Step 3: If total minimum > available: use minimums (clipped)
- [ ] Step 4: If total maximum <= available AND expand: use maximums
- [ ] Step 5: If total minimum < available < total maximum: distribute
- [ ] Step 6: Account for borders and padding in available width

### expand_widths() - Distributing Extra Space
- [ ] Calculate extra = available - current_total
- [ ] Distribute by ratio (ratio_distribute algorithm)
- [ ] Columns without ratio get default ratio = 1
- [ ] Columns with ratio = 0 get no extra space
- [ ] Verify: sum of distributed = extra exactly (no rounding loss)

### collapse_widths() - Reducing Widths
- [ ] Calculate excess = current_total - available
- [ ] Calculate reducible space per column = current - minimum
- [ ] Distribute reduction proportionally to reducible space
- [ ] Never go below minimum
- [ ] When column hits minimum, redistribute to others
- [ ] Verify: sum of reductions = excess exactly

## Test Vectors (from spec)
```
Test 1: expand_widths

```

+{"id":"rich_rust-q5f","title":"Validate Table Width Algorithms against RICH_SPEC Section 9.4","description":"## Objective
Verify Table width calculation algorithms match RICH_SPEC.md Section 9.4 EXACTLY.

## Background
This is the CRITICAL algorithm validation for Table. Width calculation is complex and must match spec precisely to avoid layout bugs.

## Validation Checklist

### get_row_widths()
- [ ] Measures each cell in row
- [ ] Returns Vec<Measurement> with min/max per column
- [ ] Accounts for padding
- [ ] Handles multi-line cells correctly

### calculate_widths() Main Algorithm
- [ ] Step 1: Get minimum and maximum widths per column
- [ ] Step 2: Calculate total minimum width
- [ ] Step 3: If total minimum > available: use minimums (clipped)
- [ ] Step 4: If total maximum <= available AND expand: use maximums
- [ ] Step 5: If total minimum < available < total maximum: distribute
- [ ] Step 6: Account for borders and padding in available width

### expand_widths() - Distributing Extra Space
- [ ] Calculate extra = available - current_total
- [ ] Distribute by ratio (ratio_distribute algorithm)
- [ ] Columns without ratio get default ratio = 1
- [ ] Columns with ratio = 0 get no extra space
- [ ] Verify: sum of distributed = extra exactly (no rounding loss)

### collapse_widths() - Reducing Widths
- [ ] Calculate excess = current_total - available
- [ ] Calculate reducible space per column = current - minimum
- [ ] Distribute reduction proportionally to reducible space
- [ ] Never go below minimum
- [ ] When column hits minimum, redistribute to others
- [ ] Verify: sum of reductions = excess exactly

## Test Vectors (from spec)
```
Test 1: expand_widths
  Input:  widths=[20,20,20], ratios=[1,2,1], available=100
  Expect: [30,40,30]  (extra 40 distributed 1:2:1)

Test 2: collapse_widths
  Input:  widths=[40,30,30], minimums=[10,10,10], available=70
  Expect: ~[27,21,22] (excess 30 reduced proportionally)

Test 3: boundary
  Input:  widths=[10,10,10], minimums=[10,10,10], available=20
  Expect: [10,10,10] (can't go below minimum, clipped)
```

## Logging Requirements
When running tests, log:
- Input widths, minimums, maximums
- Available width
- Each step of the algorithm
- Final output widths
- Verification that sum matches

## Files to Examine
- src/renderables/table.rs (width calculation functions)
- RICH_SPEC.md Section 9.4","acceptance_criteria":"Width algorithms match spec EXACTLY. All test vectors pass. Logs show calculation steps.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:46:41.664745764-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T13:09:03.895164452-05:00","labels":["phase-1","validation"]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-qgs","title":"Validate Markup Parser against RICH_SPEC Section 4","description":"## Objective
Verify the Markup parser in src/markup/mod.rs matches RICH_SPEC.md Section 4 exactly.

## Background
The markup parser converts Rich-style markup like '[bold red]Hello[/]' into styled segments. It's the primary user-facing API for styling text. The parser handles nested tags, escaping, and error recovery.

## Validation Checklist

### 4.1 Tag Syntax
- [ ] Verify opening tag: [style] where style is parsed by Style::parse
- [ ] Verify closing tag: [/] closes most recent tag
- [ ] Verify named close: [/bold] closes specific tag
- [ ] Verify escape sequence: backslash-bracket produces literal bracket

### 4.2 ParseElement Enum
- [ ] Verify variants: Text(String), Open(Tag), Close(Option<String>)
- [ ] Verify Tag struct contains: name (String), style (Style)

### 4.3 Parser Behavior
- [ ] Verify render(markup) returns Vec<Segment>
- [ ] Verify nested tags: [bold][red]text[/][/] applies both styles
- [ ] Verify style inheritance: inner tags combine with outer
- [ ] Verify unmatched close tags are treated as literal text
- [ ] Verify unclosed tags at end are auto-closed

### 4.4 Error Handling (MarkupError)
- [ ] Verify InvalidTag for malformed tags
- [ ] Verify UnmatchedClose for close without open
- [ ] Verify error recovery: invalid markup rendered as literal

### 4.5 Complex Cases
- [ ] Test: [bold]a[italic]b[/italic]c[/bold] - proper nesting
- [ ] Test: [red]a[blue]b[/]c[/] - color override then restore
- [ ] Test: [link=URL]click[/] - hyperlink
- [ ] Test: escaped brackets - literal brackets
- [ ] Test: [bold red on blue]text[/] - compound style

## Files to Examine
- src/markup/mod.rs (primary)
- src/style.rs (Style::parse)
- RICH_SPEC.md Section 4","acceptance_criteria":"All checklist items verified. Parser handles all edge cases correctly.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:17:05.871978582-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:17:05.871978582-05:00","labels":["phase-1","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-qgs","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-q1k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:19:35.801842899-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

- [ ] Verify control segments produce correct escape codes

### 7.6 Recording (if implemented)

- [ ] Verify export_html() returns HTML (if implemented)

 {"id":"rich_rust-tkj","title":"Set Up CI/CD Pipeline with Test Artifacts","description":"## Objective
Create CI/CD pipeline that runs all tests and preserves artifacts for debugging.

## Background
CI is essential for catching regressions before merge. The pipeline must run unit tests, E2E tests, and benchmarks, preserving logs and artifacts for debugging failures.

## Implementation Requirements

### GitHub Actions Workflow (.github/workflows/ci.yml)
```yaml
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main, master]
  pull_request:

env:
  CARGO_TERM_COLOR: always
  RUST_LOG: debug
  RUST_BACKTRACE: 1

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Install Rust
        uses: dtolnay/rust-action@stable
        
      - name: Cache cargo
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: |
            ~/.cargo/registry
            ~/.cargo/git
            target
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-cargo-${{ hashFiles('**/Cargo.lock') }}
          
      - name: Run unit tests
        run: cargo test --all-features -- --nocapture 2>&1 | tee test-output.log
        
      - name: Run E2E tests
        run: cargo test --test '*' -- --nocapture 2>&1 | tee e2e-output.log
        
      - name: Upload test logs
        if: always()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: test-logs
          path: |
            test-output.log
            e2e-output.log
          retention-days: 30
          
  benchmarks:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Run benchmarks
        run: cargo bench -- --noplot 2>&1 | tee bench-output.log
        
      - name: Upload benchmark results
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: benchmark-results
          path: |
            bench-output.log
            target/criterion
          retention-days: 90
          
  coverage:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Install cargo-tarpaulin
        run: cargo install cargo-tarpaulin
        
      - name: Generate coverage
        run: cargo tarpaulin --out Xml --output-dir coverage
        
      - name: Upload coverage
        uses: codecov/codecov-action@v4
        with:
          files: coverage/cobertura.xml
```

### Artifact Preservation
- Test output logs (30 days)
- Benchmark results (90 days)
- Coverage reports
- Golden file diffs on failure

### Failure Notifications
- GitHub status checks
- Optional: Slack/email on failure

## Files to Create
- .github/workflows/ci.yml
- .github/workflows/benchmarks.yml (optional separate)","acceptance_criteria":"CI runs on every PR. Artifacts preserved. Coverage tracked.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:47:45.408661933-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:47:45.408661933-05:00","labels":["ci","infrastructure"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-tkj","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-6d6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:48:04.393233497-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

 {"id":"rich_rust-xz9","title":"Add Unit Tests for Markup Parser","description":"## Objective
Comprehensive unit test coverage for src/markup/mod.rs.

## Test Cases Required

### Basic Parsing
- [ ] Plain text (no markup)
- [ ] Single tag: [bold]text[/]
- [ ] Self-closing style application
- [ ] Named close: [/bold]

### Nested Tags
- [ ] [bold][italic]text[/][/]
- [ ] [bold][italic]text[/italic][/bold]
- [ ] Deep nesting (3+ levels)
- [ ] Sibling tags

### Style Inheritance
- [ ] Inner tag inherits outer
- [ ] Inner color overrides outer
- [ ] Attributes combine

### Escaping
- [ ] \[ produces [
- [ ] \] produces ]
- [ ] \\ produces \
- [ ] Mixed escaped and tags

### Error Cases
- [ ] Unclosed tag at EOF
- [ ] Unmatched close tag
- [ ] Invalid style in tag
- [ ] Empty tag []
- [ ] Malformed tag [bold
- [ ] No panic on any input

### Complex Cases
- [ ] [link=URL]text[/]
- [ ] [bold red on blue]text[/]
- [ ] Multiple tags same line
- [ ] Tags with whitespace

## Files to Create/Modify
- src/markup/mod.rs (expand tests section)","acceptance_criteria":"100% test coverage for markup parser. No panics on any input.","notes":"## Logging Requirements

Markup parser tests must log the parsing process:

1. **Input**: The markup string being parsed
2. **Tokens**: Each token as it's recognized
3. **Stack**: Tag stack state during parsing
4. **Output**: Final segments with styles

Example:
```rust
#[test]
fn test_nested_markup() {
    let _guard = init_test_logging();
    
    let input = "[bold][red]hello[/red][/bold]";
    info!(input = %input, "Parsing markup");
    
    let segments = render(input);
    
    for (i, seg) in segments.iter().enumerate() {
        debug!(
            index = i,
            text = %seg.text,
            style = ?seg.style,
            "Segment"
        );
    }
    
    assert_eq!(segments.len(), 1);
    let style = segments[0].style.as_ref().unwrap();
    assert!(style.attributes.is_set(Attributes::BOLD));
    
    info!("Nested markup PASSED");
}
```","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:24:11.457599519-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:46:03.296298648-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-xz9","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-33q","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:24:25.769330717-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

 {"id":"rich_rust-yz6","title":"EPIC: Performance Optimization","description":"## Objective
Optimize rich_rust for production performance, focusing on hot paths identified through profiling.

## Background
Terminal rendering can be performance-critical in CLI tools. Key optimizations include:
1. Caching frequently computed values
2. Minimizing allocations in hot paths
3. Efficient string building for ANSI output
4. Lazy evaluation where possible

## Scope

### Caching (High Priority)
- LRU cache for color parsing
- Cell width caching for repeated strings
- Style ANSI code caching

### Allocation Reduction
- Reuse buffers in rendering loop
- SmallVec for small collections
- String interning for common values

### Algorithm Optimization
- Table width calculation
- Text wrapping
- Segment merging

### Benchmarks
- Establish baseline benchmarks
- Measure improvement after each optimization
- Prevent regressions

## Success Criteria
- Measurable improvement in benchmark suite
- No performance regressions
- Acceptable memory footprint","acceptance_criteria":"Benchmarks show improvement. No regressions.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:24:26.604866419-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:24:26.604866419-05:00"}
 {"id":"rich_rust-zbw","title":"Create Regression Test Suite","description":"## Objective
Create dedicated regression test suite to catch behavioral changes.

## Background
Regressions are bugs that reintroduce previously fixed issues or change expected behavior. A regression suite specifically tests known past issues and edge cases.

## Implementation

### Regression Test Structure
```
tests/
  regression/
    mod.rs           # Regression test module
    issue_001.rs     # Named after issue/bug
    issue_002.rs
    ...
```

### Regression Test Format
Each regression test must document:
1. What bug it prevents
2. When it was introduced
3. What the fix was
4. How to reproduce

```rust
/// Regression test for issue #42
/// 
/// Bug: Table width calculation overflowed when sum of minimums exceeded usize::MAX / 2
/// Fixed: 2024-01-15 in commit abc123
/// 
/// This test ensures the fix remains in place.
#[test]
fn regression_issue_42_table_width_overflow() {
    let _guard = init_test_logging();
    info!("Regression test: issue #42 - table width overflow");
    
    // Reproduce the conditions that caused the bug
    let table = Table::new()
        .add_column(Column::new("A").min_width(usize::MAX / 4))
        .add_column(Column::new("B").min_width(usize::MAX / 4))
        .add_column(Column::new("C").min_width(usize::MAX / 4));
    
    // This should not panic or overflow
    let result = std::panic::catch_unwind(|| {
        table.calculate_widths(100);
    });
    
    assert!(result.is_ok(), "Table width calculation panicked");
    info!("Regression test PASSED");
}
```

### Categories
1. **Parsing regressions**: Color, style, markup parsing edge cases
2. **Layout regressions**: Table width, text wrap, alignment
3. **Rendering regressions**: ANSI output, Unicode handling
4. **Performance regressions**: Speed, memory usage

### Running Regression Tests
```bash
# Run all regression tests
cargo test regression --features all

# Run specific category
cargo test regression::parsing
```

## Files to Create
- tests/regression/mod.rs
- tests/regression/*.rs (one per regression)","acceptance_criteria":"Regression suite established. Each test documents the bug it prevents.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:47:46.701299753-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:47:46.701299753-05:00","labels":["regression","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-zbw","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-6d6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:48:04.344645233-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"rich_rust-zbw","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-4ot","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T11:11:54.149145789-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-zca","title":"Add Unit Tests for Text Module","description":"## Objective
Comprehensive unit test coverage for src/text.rs.

## Test Cases Required

### Text Construction
- [ ] Text::new()
- [ ] Text::plain(text)
- [ ] Text::styled(text, style)
- [ ] From<String> and From<&str>

### Text Manipulation
- [ ] append() adds plain text
- [ ] append_styled() adds styled text
- [ ] stylize() applies span
- [ ] Overlapping spans
- [ ] Adjacent spans

### Text Rendering
- [ ] render() produces segments
- [ ] Styles applied correctly
- [ ] Tab expansion
- [ ] Base style + spans

### Text Wrapping
- [ ] wrap() at word boundaries
- [ ] wrap() breaks long words
- [ ] wrap() respects overflow mode
- [ ] wrap() with wide characters
- [ ] no_wrap flag

### Text Division (CRITICAL)
- [ ] divide() basic split
- [ ] divide() with spans crossing boundaries
- [ ] divide() span starts at cut
- [ ] divide() span ends at cut
- [ ] divide() multiple spans
- [ ] divide() empty divisions

### Justification
- [ ] Left justify
- [ ] Right justify
- [ ] Center justify
- [ ] Full justify

## Files to Create/Modify
- src/text.rs (expand tests section)","acceptance_criteria":"100% test coverage for text.rs. divide() thoroughly tested.","notes":"## Logging Requirements

Text module tests are CRITICAL and must have extensive logging:

1. **Text construction**: Log plain text and initial spans
2. **Stylize operations**: Log start/end positions and style
3. **Wrap operations**: Log width, input length, output line count
4. **Divide operations**: Log offsets and resulting parts WITH spans

Example for divide() - the most critical test:
```rust
#[test]
fn test_text_divide_with_spans() {
    let _guard = init_test_logging();
    let span = info_span!("test_divide_spans");
    let _enter = span.enter();
    
    let mut text = Text::plain("hello world!");
    info!(plain = %text.plain, "Initial text");
    
    text.stylize(6, 11, Style::new().bold());
    debug!(spans = ?text.spans, "After stylize");
    
    let parts = text.divide(&[5, 10]);
    for (i, part) in parts.iter().enumerate() {
        debug!(
            index = i,
            plain = %part.plain,
            spans = ?part.spans,
            "Divided part"
        );
    }
    
    // Detailed assertions with logging
    assert_eq!(parts[0].plain, "hello", "Part 0 content");
    assert!(parts[0].spans.is_empty(), "Part 0 should have no spans");
    debug!(part = 0, "Verified: no spans");
    
    assert_eq!(parts[1].plain, " worl", "Part 1 content");
    assert!(!parts[1].spans.is_empty(), "Part 1 should have span");
    debug!(part = 1, span = ?parts[1].spans[0], "Verified: has span");
    
    info!("Text divide with spans PASSED");
}
```","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:23:47.51982787-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:46:02.359875505-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-zca","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-33q","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:24:24.783337024-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

@@ -0,0 +1,104 @@
+name: CI
+
+on:
+  push:
+    branches: [main, master]
+  pull_request:
+
+env:
+  CARGO_TERM_COLOR: always
+  RUSTFLAGS: -D warnings
+
+jobs:
+  check:
+    name: Check
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
+
+      - name: Install Rust toolchain
+        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@nightly
+        with:
+          components: rustfmt, clippy
+
+      - name: Cache cargo registry and build
+        uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
+
+      - name: Check formatting
+        run: cargo fmt --all -- --check
+
+      - name: Run Clippy (no features)
+        run: cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings
+
+      - name: Run Clippy (all features)
+        run: cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings
+
+      - name: Build (no features)
+        run: cargo build --all-targets
+
+      - name: Build (all features)
+        run: cargo build --all-targets --all-features
+
+      - name: Run tests (no features)
+        run: cargo test
+
+      - name: Run tests (all features)
+        run: cargo test --all-features
+
+  feature-matrix:
+    name: Test Features (${{ matrix.features }})
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    strategy:
+      fail-fast: false
+      matrix:
+        features:
+          - ""
+          - "syntax"
+          - "markdown"
+          - "json"
+          - "full"
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
+
+      - name: Install Rust toolchain
+        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@nightly
+
+      - name: Cache cargo registry and build
+        uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
+        with:
+          key: ${{ matrix.features }}
+
+      - name: Build with features
+        run: |
+          if [ -z "${{ matrix.features }}" ]; then
+            cargo build --all-targets
+          else
+            cargo build --all-targets --features "${{ matrix.features }}"
+          fi
+
+      - name: Test with features
+        run: |
+          if [ -z "${{ matrix.features }}" ]; then
+            cargo test
+          else
+            cargo test --features "${{ matrix.features }}"
+          fi
+
+  test-os:
+    name: Test (${{ matrix.os }})
+    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
+    strategy:
+      fail-fast: false
+      matrix:
+        os: [ubuntu-latest, macos-latest, windows-latest]
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
+
+      - name: Install Rust toolchain
+        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@nightly
+
+      - name: Cache cargo registry and build
+        uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
+
+      - name: Run tests
+        run: cargo test --all-features

@@ -0,0 +1,149 @@
+name: Release
+
+on:
+  push:
+    tags:
+      - 'v*'
+
+env:
+  CARGO_TERM_COLOR: always
+  CRATE_NAME: rich_rust
+
+permissions:
+  contents: write
+
+jobs:
+  build:
+    name: Build (${{ matrix.target }})
+    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
+    strategy:
+      fail-fast: false
+      matrix:
+        include:
+          - target: x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu
+            os: ubuntu-latest
+            artifact_name: rich_rust-linux-x64
+          - target: aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu
+            os: ubuntu-latest
+            artifact_name: rich_rust-linux-arm64
+          - target: x86_64-apple-darwin
+            os: macos-latest
+            artifact_name: rich_rust-macos-x64
+          - target: aarch64-apple-darwin
+            os: macos-latest
+            artifact_name: rich_rust-macos-arm64
+          - target: x86_64-pc-windows-msvc
+            os: windows-latest
+            artifact_name: rich_rust-windows-x64
+
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
+
+      - name: Install Rust toolchain
+        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@nightly
+        with:
+          targets: ${{ matrix.target }}
+
+      - name: Install cross-compilation tools (Linux ARM64)
+        if: matrix.target == 'aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu'
+        run: |
+          sudo apt-get update
+          sudo apt-get install -y gcc-aarch64-linux-gnu
+
+      - name: Cache cargo registry and build
+        uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
+        with:
+          key: ${{ matrix.target }}
+
+      - name: Build release library
+        run: cargo build --release --all-features --target ${{ matrix.target }}
+        env:
+          CARGO_TARGET_AARCH64_UNKNOWN_LINUX_GNU_LINKER: aarch64-linux-gnu-gcc
+
+      - name: Prepare artifact (Unix)
+        if: runner.os != 'Windows'
+        run: |
+          mkdir -p dist
+          # Copy static library if it exists
+          if [ -f "target/${{ matrix.target }}/release/lib${{ env.CRATE_NAME }}.a" ]; then
+            cp "target/${{ matrix.target }}/release/lib${{ env.CRATE_NAME }}.a" "dist/${{ matrix.artifact_name }}.a"
+          fi
+          # Copy dynamic library if it exists
+          if [ -f "target/${{ matrix.target }}/release/lib${{ env.CRATE_NAME }}.so" ]; then
+            cp "target/${{ matrix.target }}/release/lib${{ env.CRATE_NAME }}.so" "dist/${{ matrix.artifact_name }}.so"
+          fi
+          if [ -f "target/${{ matrix.target }}/release/lib${{ env.CRATE_NAME }}.dylib" ]; then
+            cp "target/${{ matrix.target }}/release/lib${{ env.CRATE_NAME }}.dylib" "dist/${{ matrix.artifact_name }}.dylib"
+          fi
+          # Create tarball of the rlib
+          tar -czvf "dist/${{ matrix.artifact_name }}.tar.gz" -C "target/${{ matrix.target }}/release" .
+
+      - name: Prepare artifact (Windows)
+        if: runner.os == 'Windows'
+        shell: pwsh
+        run: |
+          New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path dist
+          # Copy lib file if exists
+          $libPath = "target/${{ matrix.target }}/release/${{ env.CRATE_NAME }}.lib"
+          if (Test-Path $libPath) {
+            Copy-Item $libPath "dist/${{ matrix.artifact_name }}.lib"
+          }
+          # Copy dll if exists
+          $dllPath = "target/${{ matrix.target }}/release/${{ env.CRATE_NAME }}.dll"
+          if (Test-Path $dllPath) {
+            Copy-Item $dllPath "dist/${{ matrix.artifact_name }}.dll"
+          }
+          # Create zip of release directory
+          Compress-Archive -Path "target/${{ matrix.target }}/release/*" -DestinationPath "dist/${{ matrix.artifact_name }}.zip"
+
+      - name: Upload artifact
+        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
+        with:
+          name: ${{ matrix.artifact_name }}
+          path: dist/*
+          if-no-files-found: warn
+
+  release:
+    name: Create Release
+    needs: build
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
+
+      - name: Download all artifacts
+        uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
+        with:
+          path: artifacts
+
+      - name: Prepare release assets
+        run: |
+          mkdir -p release
+          # Move all artifacts to release directory
+          find artifacts -type f \( -name "*.tar.gz" -o -name "*.zip" -o -name "*.a" -o -name "*.so" -o -name "*.dylib" -o -name "*.lib" -o -name "*.dll" \) -exec mv {} release/ \;
+          ls -la release/
+
+      - name: Generate checksums
+        run: |
+          cd release
+          if ls * 1> /dev/null 2>&1; then
+            sha256sum * > SHA256SUMS.txt
+            cat SHA256SUMS.txt
+          else
+            echo "No release files found"
+          fi
+
+      - name: Extract version from tag
+        id: version
+        run: echo "VERSION=${GITHUB_REF#refs/tags/}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
+
+      - name: Create GitHub Release
+        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
+        with:
+          name: Release ${{ steps.version.outputs.VERSION }}
+          draft: false
+          prerelease: ${{ contains(github.ref, '-') }}
+          generate_release_notes: true
+          files: |
+            release/*
+        env:
+          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
